rationale: In the citing paper, this citation is used in the theory-building section to justify a key premise behind a hypothesis, not just in a generic literature list. The authors explicitly connect the cited work to their claim that consumers draw attribute inferences under missing information, then extend that logic to greenness judgments from price cues. Although cited alongside another paper and not reused methodologically, it plays a meaningful conceptual role in framing predictions.
relation_description: The 2017 paper uses Chernev and Carpenter (2001) as prior evidence that consumers infer unobserved attributes from available cues when information is incomplete. It applies this inference logic to argue that consumers may infer greater environmental commitment from a hotel's higher price image.
